channel.title: SEAtoday - Learning & Education
channel.description: Upcoming events for SEAtoday - Learning & Education

item.title: Coworking Open House Week @ The Cloud Room
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: The Cloud Room, 1424 11th Ave Suite 400, Seattle, WA

Curious about coworking and looking for the perfect spot to get work done and reach your goals? Join us for a complimentary day during our upcoming Open House Week and experience what it’s like to be a part of our incredible community! What's Included: One Free Trial Day: Pick any day during Open House Week to try out our coworking space, free of charge! Arrive anytime 9am or later — whatever works best with your schedule. Access to our beautiful workspace including quiet desk space with monitors, large lounge area, phone booths, bar, and outdoor patio. Complimentary coffee, tea, snacks and kombucha. High-speed Wi-Fi, printing, and meeting rooms available for rent The chance to meet like-minded professionals and get a feel for the Cloud Room vibe. Special Offer: If you love your free day with us and decide to become a member, we’ll waive your initiation fees when you sign up! How to Join: Reserve your free trial day by selecting one day of your choice on our Calendly \(one free trial day per person\) Show up and enjoy your day at Cloud Room! Be sure to check in with the staff at the front of the lounge so we can give you a quick tour of the space and get you set up with the wifi. Sign up for membership to take advantage of waived initiation fees. Don’t miss this chance to test out our space, meet amazing people, and see if Cloud Room is the perfect fit for your work needs! Current Promotions: ⭐️ Sign up for a grounded membership and get your first month free \+ 10% off you first year of membership. 3 month commitment required. ⭐️ Floating Membership Promo: Take 50% off your first full month of floating membership. Spaces are limited—RSVP today.

Event details

item.title: Spy Maters Camp II
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Suncatcher Forest School, 18701 se 60th st, Issaquah, WA

Calling all young secret agents! This time kids are welcome to join action-packed Outdoor Spy Camp, where kids will train to become top-notch spies through thrilling challenges, daring missions, and teamwork! Obstacle Courses & Ninja Training – Agents-in-training will test their agility, speed, and stealth as they navigate exciting ninja courses, crawl through the forest and learn to use nature objects to complete the challenge. Scavenger Hunts & Riddle Solving – Spies must sharpen their detective skills by decoding secret messages, solving riddles, and uncovering hidden clues to complete their top-secret missions. Nerf Battles & Tactical Challenges – Gear up for friendly Nerf wars, where teamwork and strategy are key to outsmarting the opposition. Agents will also practice their precision and accuracy with target practice and stealth maneuvers. Escape Room-Style Puzzles – Secret agents will work together to crack codes, unlock treasure chests, and solve mind-bending puzzles before time runs out! Outdoor Adventure & Camouflage Training – Spies will blend into their surroundings, practice silent movement, and use nature to their advantage while completing their missions. This high-energy camp is perfect for kids who love adventure, problem-solving, and action-packed fun in the great outdoors! Are you ready to accept the mission? - follow the link to book a spot!

Event details

item.title: La Hora de Cuentos Familiares
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 10:30 AM

Monday, April 13, 2026, 10:30 - 11am Durante la hora de cuentos, los niños de 0 a 5 años y sus familias pueden disfrutar de historias, canciones y divertidas actividades de aprendizaje. Evento en español. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Área para niños Registration Required: No se requiere inscripción. Detailed Description: El aprendizaje temprano es una excelente manera para que los niños de 0 a 5 años y sus familias desarrollen habilidades de aprendizaje, alfabetización y lenguaje, así como una comunidad con otras familias., Leemos cuentos, cantamos canciones y tenemos actividades educativas atractivas que ayudan a los niños y sus cuidadores a crecer juntos., -------------------------------------------------------, At storytime, kids ages 0-5 and their families can enjoy stories, songs, and fun learning activities., Early Learning is a great way for kids ages 0-5 and their families to develop learning, literacy, and language skills as well as community with other families., We read stories, sing songs, and have engaging, educational activities that help kids and their caregivers grow together. Event Logistics: Este evento se llevará a cabo en español., No se requiere inscripción, pero estos programas son muy populares y la capacidad de la sala puede llenarse rápidamente., Las familias de todas las edades son bienvenidas, aunque este programa de aprendizaje temprano está diseñado para niños de 0 a 5 años, sus cuidadores y familias., Obtenga más información sobre los programas Aprendizaje temprano y la hora de cuentos ., -------------------------------------------------------, This event will take place in Spanish., Registration is not required but these programs are very popular and the room capacity can fill up quickly., Families of all ages are welcome, though this early learning program is intended for youth aged 0-5, their caregivers and families.Find out more about Early Learning & Story Time programs. Contact Us: Si tiene alguna pregunta sobre este evento, pregúntenos o llame al 206-386-4636., -------------------------------------------------------, If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: Podemos ofrecer acomodamiento para personas con discapacidades en los eventos de la biblioteca. Póngase encontacto con LEAP al menos siete días antes del evento para solicitar acomodamiento., Todos los programas de la biblioteca son gratuitos y abiertos al público., -------------------------------------------------------, We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations.All Library programs are free and open to the public.

Event details

item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 11 AM

Monday, April 13, 2026, 11am - 5pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 5 - Mixing Chamber Meeting Room Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Drop-in tax help is offered on a first-come, first-served basis., This service is free and available to individuals only., The free service is not available for business tax returns., To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required Documents: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return, Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return, Recommended: Last year's tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents will be accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov. , You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner United Way of King County . Event Logistics: United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025 \(or less than $96,000 for non-single filers\)., For eligibility details, contact United Way at 206-461-3700. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

Event details

item.title: AI Practitioner Part 1: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: MAKERS, 92 Lenora Street, Seattle, WA

Monday, April 13, 2026, 1 - 4pm In this AI foundational 6-part series, you will learn fundamental concepts and terminology related to artificial intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 4 - Room 4 Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: Explore the relationships between these different AI approaches and understand how to distinguish scenarios suited for traditional software methods versus AI/ML techniques., This session covers key AWS AI services and their capabilities, including Amazon SageMaker, Amazon Bedrock, and pre-trained AI services. You will be introduced to foundation models, how they work, and important considerations when using them, such as potential risks and strategies for optimizing outputs. By the end of the session, you will have a broad understanding of AI concepts, AWS tools for AI development, and foundational knowledge to continue learning about generative AI and responsible AI practices., In this session, you will learn to: Define artificial intelligence and machine learning terminology, Explain the relationship between the concepts of AI, machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI, Distinguish between scenarios that are suited for traditional software methods, traditional ML methods, or generative AI, Identify the capabilities of AWS managed AI/ML services, Explain how tokens, vectors, and embeddings are used in a generative AI model, Describe the effect of model parameters on model outputs, Discuss the factors to consider when selecting a foundation model, This session is intended for one or more of the following: Individuals who are ready to learn about AWS AI related technologies, Individuals in both technical and non-technical roles, Individuals looking reskill for the AWS Cloud, Business decision makers, It is recommended that you have the following general skills: Understanding of AWS global infrastructure, Understanding of AWS networking services and how to build a functioning Amazon VPC solution, Understanding of Amazon S3, Amazon EBS, and Amazon EFS, Understanding of AWS security services, Understanding of virtual computer services using Amazon EC2, These skills can be gained by taking the following AWS Skills Center session: AWS Skills Centers: Becoming a Cloud Practitioner - Parts 1-4, Thanks to our partner AWS Skills Center Seattle . Event Logistics: The program aligns with concepts covered in the AWS Certified AI Practitioner exam but is designed as an introductory overview rather than exam preparation. The classes are led by AWS Certified Instructors and in partnership with the AWS Skills Centers . Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

Event details

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, April 13, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

Event details

item.title: Virtual & In-Person Citizenship Class - English & Vietnamese
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 9 AM

Tuesday, April 14, 2026, 9 - 11am Lớp học này được tạo ra dành cho những người cần giúp đỡ để chuẩn bị cho bài thi quốc tịch của họ. Chương trình song ngữ bằng tiếng Việt và tiếng Anh. --------------------------------------- This class is designed for individuals that need assistance in preparing for their citizenship exam. Bilingual event in Vietnamese/English. Location Link: non-library location Registration Required: Registration is required / Bắt buộc phải đăng ký. Detailed Description: Lớp Học Chuẩn Bị Thi Quốc Tịch Trực Tuyến - Tiếng Việt & Tiếng AnhLớp học vào các ngày Thứ Ba và Thứ Năm trong 11tuần, bắt đầu từ 4/7/2026 và kết thúc vào 6/18/2026. Các lớp học gặp mặt trực tiếp tại ACRS; Các lớp học thứ năm là ảo. Người dạy là một giảng viên giàu kinh nghiệm, giảng dạy bằng cả tiếng Việt và tiếng Anh., Lớp học này được tạo ra dành cho những người cần giúp đỡ để chuẩn bị cho bài thi quốc tịch của họ.; lớp được cung cấp bởi tổ chức Asian Counseling and Referral Service ., ---------------------------------------, The class meets Tuesdays and Thursdays for 11 weeks, starting on April 7 and ending on June 18. Tuesday classes meet in-person at ACRS; Thursday classes are virtual. It is taught by an experienced instructor in Vietnamese and English., This class is designed for individuals that need assistance in preparing for their citizenship exam; the class is provided by Asian Counseling and Referral Service., Thanks to our partner Asian Counseling and Referral Service . Event Logistics: Bắt buộc phải đăng ký để tham gia. Hãy bấm vào đây để đăng ký., Location: 3639 Martin Luther King Jr Way S \#106, Seattle, WA 98108 , Học viên phải nộp bản sao thẻ xanh để ghi danh theo học lớp này., Để tham gia vào lớp học này, quý vị cần có một kết nối mạng internet và một thiết bị để vào mạng internet \(điện thoại thông minh, máy laptop, máy tính bàn, hoặc máy tính bảng\) với phần mềm gọi họp qua mạng Microsoft Teams được cài đặt sẵn, và quý vị sẽ cần một địa chỉ email để đăng ký tham gia., Nếu quý vị cần trợ giúp trong việc tạo một địa chỉ email hoặc cài đặt phần mềm Microsoft Teams, xin liên lạc với bộ phận trợ giúp của tổ chức TechConnect Washington Community trước khi lớp học bắt đầu. Trợ giúp về công nghệ có sẵn trong các ngôn ngữ: tiếng Afaan Oromoo, tiếng Amharic, tiếng Anh, tiếng Sidamo, tiếng Tây Ban Nha, tiếng Tigrinya, tiếng Việt, và tiếng Wolaytta, 7 ngày trong một tuần từ 8h sáng đến 8h tối bằng điện thoại 1-800-216-1132 , đối thoại qua mạng \(web chat\), và qua email tại địa chỉ techconnect@eec-wa.org ., Tìm hiểu thêm về Hỗ trợ công dân. , ---------------------------------------, Registration is required.Register here ., Asian Counseling and Referral Service 3639 Martin Luther King Jr Way S \#106, Seattle, WA 98108 , Students are required to submit a copy of their green card in order to register for this class., , To take part in the class you will need an internet connection and a device to connect to the internet \(smartphone, laptop, desktop, or tablet computer\) with the Microsoft Teams web/video conferencing platform installed, and you will need an email address to register., If you need help getting an email address or installing or using Microsoft Teams, contact the TechConnect Washington Community helpdesk before the class is scheduled to begin., Technical support is available in Afaan Oromoo, Amharic, English, Sidamo, Spanish, Tigrinya, Vietnamese, and Wolaytta, 7 days a week between 8 am - 8 pm by phone \(800\) 216-1132 , web chat , and email to techconnect@eec-wa.org ., Find out more about Citizenship Help . Contact Us: Nếu quý vị có thắc mắc về lớp học, hãy liên hệ với giảng viên Duy Ngo qua email duyn@acrs.org hoặc gọi \(206\) 705-8186.---------------------------------------, If you have questions about the class, contact the instructor Duy Ngo via email at duyn@acrs.org , call or textmessage \(206\) 705-8186. ADA Accommodations: Chúng tôi có thể cung cấp hỗ trợ cho những người cần trợ giúp khuyết tật tại các chương trình của Thư Viện. Xin vui lòng liên hệ với LEAP để yêu cầu trợ giúp ít nhất bảy ngày trước khi chương trình diễn ra., Tất cả các chương trình của Thư viện đều miễn phí và mở cửa cho công chúng.---------------------------------------, We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

item.title: Coworking Tuesdays
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: Bothell Kenmore Chamber, 10031 Main Street, Suite A, Bothell, WA

Tuesday Cowork \+ Coffee/Tea \(Weekly\) Need a reason to get out of your inbox cave and into a productive, low-pressure space? Join us every Tuesday for a drop-in coworking morning—bring your laptop, knock out your priorities, and enjoy the energy of working alongside other local professionals. When: Tuesdays | 9:30–11:30 AM | No registration needed Format: Come for the full two hours or pop in when you can Who it’s for: Business owners, remote workers, creatives, students, nonprofit leaders—anyone who wants focused work time \+ light community What to expect: Quiet “get-it-done” work blocks, optional hellos \+ quick intros, and a friendly vibe \(no forced networking\) Location \(through February\): Bothell Visitor Center We’ll rotate locations monthly—same time, same Tuesday rhythm, fresh spaces. Bring: Your laptop \+ whatever you need to work. We have the coffee, tea, and water! Questions? Email sandra@bothellkenmorechamber.org

item.title: Job Interview Essentials
item.description:
Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 1:30 PM

Location: Northwest Worklofts (Commons), 107 West Denny Way, Seattle, WA

Tuesday, April 14, 2026, 1:30 - 3pm This interactive presentation covers essential techniques to help you make a positive and lasting impression at your next job interview. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 4 - Room 2 Registration Required: Registration is not required. So do you want to learn about what resources are available to you to maximize this time? Register to learn more now. These quarterly events are brought to you by the Seattle Southside Chamber of Commerce and Explore Seattle Southside. This meeting is a pivotal opportunity for stakeholders to collaborate and ensure our region is fully prepared to host FIFA events successfully. Together, we can create a unified and effective execution plan that benefits all.

item.title: Free Tax Help
item.description:
Wednesday, April 15, 2026 at 10 AM

Wednesday, April 15, 2026, 10am - 1:30pm Trained volunteers will be available to answer questions and prepare personal tax returns. Location Link: Broadview Branch Room Location: Meeting Room Detailed Description: Drop-in tax help is offered on a first-come, first-served basis. This service is free and available to individuals only. The free service is not available for business tax returns. To receive tax help, bring the following documents:, Required Documents: Photo identification, Social Security cards or Individual Taxpayer Identification Numbers \(ITIN\) for everyone listed on the tax return., Proof of income, for example, W2s and 1099s \(Social Security benefits\), Official documents on Social Security Administration letterhead, Medicare card \(if Social Security number is followed by an "A"\), Be prepared to provide information about health care coverage for each person on the return., Recommended: Last year's tax return, if available, Bank account and routing numbers for direct deposit of refunds, Photocopies or computer print-outs of any of these documents willbe accepted., Due to federal budget cuts the IRS is unable to provide the Library with certain tax forms and instruction booklets., Most IRS forms are available online at www.irs.gov ., You may make free black-and-white prints of the forms from Library computers: www.spl.org/Print , Thanks to our partner AARP . Event Logistics: For eligibility details, contact AARP at 888-227-7669., United Way Tax Help locations serve anyone who earned less than $80,000 in 2025. Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

item.title: Virtual & In-Person Citizenship Class - English & Vietnamese
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 9 AM

Thursday, April 16, 2026, 9 - 11am Lớp học này được tạo ra dành cho những người cần giúp đỡ để chuẩn bị cho bài thi quốc tịch của họ. Chương trình song ngữ bằng tiếng Việt và tiếng Anh. --------------------------------------- This class is designed for individuals that need assistance in preparing for their citizenship exam. Bilingual event in Vietnamese/English. Location Link: Online Registration Required: Registration is required / Bắt buộc phải đăng ký. Detailed Description: Lớp Học Chuẩn Bị Thi Quốc Tịch Trực Tuyến - Tiếng Việt & Tiếng AnhLớp học vào các ngày Thứ Ba và Thứ Năm trong 11tuần, bắt đầu từ 4/7/2026 và kết thúc vào 6/18/2026. Các lớp học gặp mặt trực tiếp tại ACRS; Các lớp học thứ năm là ảo. Người dạy là một giảng viên giàu kinh nghiệm, giảng dạy bằng cả tiếng Việt và tiếng Anh., Lớp học này được tạo ra dành cho những người cần giúp đỡ để chuẩn bị cho bài thi quốc tịch của họ.; lớp được cung cấp bởi tổ chức Asian Counseling and Referral Service ., ---------------------------------------, The class meets Tuesdays and Thursdays for 11 weeks, starting on April 7 and ending on June 18. Tuesday classes meet in-person at ACRS; Thursday classes are virtual. It is taught by an experienced instructor in Vietnamese and English., This class is designed for individuals that need assistance in preparing for their citizenship exam; the class is provided by Asian Counseling and Referral Service., Thanks to our partner Asian Counseling and Referral Service . Event Logistics: Bắt buộc phải đăng ký để tham gia. Hãy bấm vào đây để đăng ký.Học viên phải nộp bản sao thẻ xanh để ghi danh theo học lớp này., Để tham gia vào lớp học này, quý vị cần có một kết nối mạng internet và một thiết bị để vào mạng internet \(điện thoại thông minh, máy laptop, máy tính bàn, hoặc máy tính bảng\) với phần mềm gọi họp qua mạng Microsoft Teams được cài đặt sẵn, và quý vị sẽ cần một địa chỉ email để đăng ký tham gia., Nếu quý vị cần trợ giúp trong việc tạo một địa chỉ email hoặc cài đặt phần mềm Microsoft Teams, xin liên lạc với bộ phận trợ giúp của tổ chức TechConnect Washington Community trước khi lớp học bắt đầu. Trợ giúp về công nghệ có sẵn trong các ngôn ngữ: tiếng Afaan Oromoo, tiếng Amharic, tiếng Anh, tiếng Sidamo, tiếng Tây Ban Nha, tiếng Tigrinya, tiếng Việt, và tiếng Wolaytta, 7 ngày trong một tuần từ 8h sáng đến 8h tối bằng điện thoại 1-800-216-1132 , đối thoại qua mạng \(web chat\), và qua email tại địa chỉ techconnect@eec-wa.org ., Tìm hiểu thêm về Hỗ trợ công dân. , ---------------------------------------, Registration is required.Register here ., Students are required to submit a copy of their green card in order to register for this class., , To take part in the class you will need an internet connection and a device to connect to the internet \(smartphone, laptop, desktop, or tablet computer\) with the Microsoft Teams web/video conferencing platform installed, and you will need an email address to register., If you need help getting an email address or installing or using Microsoft Teams, contact the TechConnect Washington Community helpdesk before the class is scheduled to begin., Technical support is available in Afaan Oromoo, Amharic, English, Sidamo, Spanish, Tigrinya, Vietnamese, and Wolaytta, 7 days a week between 8 am - 8 pm by phone \(800\) 216-1132 , web chat , and email to techconnect@eec-wa.org ., Find out more about Citizenship Help . Contact Us: Nếu quý vị có thắc mắc về lớp học, hãy liên hệ với giảng viên Duy Ngo qua email duyn@acrs.org hoặc gọi \(206\) 705-8186.---------------------------------------, If you have questions about the class, contact the instructor Duy Ngo via email at duyn@acrs.org , call or textmessage \(206\) 705-8186. ADA Accommodations: Chúng tôi có thể cung cấp hỗ trợ cho những người cần trợ giúp khuyết tật tại các chương trình của Thư Viện. Xin vui lòng liên hệ với LEAP để yêu cầu trợ giúp ít nhất bảy ngày trước khi chương trình diễn ra., Tất cả các chương trình của Thư viện đều miễn phí và mở cửa cho công chúng.---------------------------------------, We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.,

item.title: Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State Exhibit
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Northwest Worklofts (Commons), 107 West Denny Way, Seattle, WA

Thursday, April 16, 2026, 10am - 6pm This exhibitconfronts how Washington courts have been sentencing people not only to prison but also to a lifetime of debt. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 8 - Gallery Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: "Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State" confronts how Washington courts have been sentencing people not only to prison but also to a lifetime of debt. The project leverages multimedia storytelling and community conversations to raise awareness about and advocate for an end to crippling court-imposed costs, fees, fines, and restitution, commonly referred to as Legal Financial Obligations, or LFOs. "Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State," goes beyond polarizing headlines and statistics, shining a light on this injustice by sharing stories of our common humanity., Thanks to our partner Living with Conviction ., Thanks to our sponsor The Seattle Public Library Foundation . Event Logistics: The gallery is open during Central Libray's open hours., Living with Conviction is a 501\(c\)\(3\) nonprofit that works to secure economic and racial justice with and by marginalized communities, especially formerly incarcerated, through community-driven research, storytelling, and legal empowerment strategies., This exhibit runs through July 26, 2026, Learn more about Exhibits . Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

Detailed Description: El aprendizaje temprano es una excelente manera para que los niños de 0 a 5 años y sus familias desarrollen habilidades de aprendizaje, alfabetización y lenguaje, así como una comunidad con otras familias., Leemos cuentos, cantamos canciones y tenemos actividades educativas atractivas que ayudan a los niños y sus cuidadores a crecer juntos., -------------------------------------------------, At storytime, kids ages 0-5 and their families can enjoy stories, songs, and fun learning activities., Early Learning is a great way for kids ages 0-5 and their families to develop learning, literacy, and language skills as well as community with other families., We read stories, sing songs, and have engaging, educational activities that help kids and their caregivers grow together., Event Logistics: Este evento se llevará a cabo en español., No se requiere inscripción, pero estos programas son muy populares y la capacidad de la sala puede llenarse rápidamente., Las familias de todas las edades son bienvenidas, aunque este programa de aprendizaje temprano está diseñado para niños de 0 a 5 años, sus cuidadores y familias., Obtenga más información sobre los programas Aprendizaje temprano y la hora de cuentos. , -------------------------------------------------------, This event will take place in Spanish., Registration is not required but these programs are very popular and the room capacity can fill up quickly., Families of all ages are welcome, though this early learning program is intended for youth aged 0-5, their caregivers and families., Find out more about Early Learning & Story Time programs. item.description:

This is a free five-hour workshop to help you meet the WA State Housing Finance Commission’s education requirements for down payment assistance and a variety of affordable housing programs and opportunities for qualified applicants! These requirements include the successful completion of a Homebuyers Education Seminar meeting the current FHA, Fannie Mae, FreddieMac, and SMBA benchmark standards.  At the conclusion of the workshop, participants will receive a certificate of achievement from the WA State Housing Finance Commission and DFI valid for two years from the date of issuance.  To join our next workshop, please complete and submit the following registration form at least 24-hours before your selected workshop date. item.title: Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State Exhibit
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Northwest Worklofts (Commons), 107 West Denny Way, Seattle, WA

Saturday, April 18, 2026, 10am - 6pm This exhibitconfronts how Washington courts have been sentencing people not only to prison but also to a lifetime of debt. Location Link: Central Library Room Location: Level 8 - Gallery Registration Required: Registration is not required. Detailed Description: "Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State" confronts how Washington courts have been sentencing people not only to prison but also to a lifetime of debt. The project leverages multimedia storytelling and community conversations to raise awareness about and advocate for an end to crippling court-imposed costs, fees, fines, and restitution, commonly referred to as Legal Financial Obligations, or LFOs. "Living with Conviction: Sentenced to Debt for Life in Washington State," goes beyond polarizing headlines and statistics, shining a light on this injustice by sharing stories of our common humanity., Thanks to our partner Living with Conviction ., Thanks to our sponsor The Seattle Public Library Foundation . Event Logistics: The gallery is open during Central Libray's open hours., Living with Conviction is a 501\(c\)\(3\) nonprofit that works to secure economic and racial justice with and by marginalized communities, especially formerly incarcerated, through community-driven research, storytelling, and legal empowerment strategies., This exhibit runs through July 26, 2026, Learn more about Exhibits . Contact Us: If you have questions about this event, Ask Us or call 206-386-4636. ADA Accommodations: We can provide accommodations for people with disabilities at Library events. Please contactLEAP at least seven days before the event to request accommodations., All Library programs are free and open to the public.

item.title: Ragamala Indian Classical Music and Arts Celebration
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Olympic Sculpture Park, 2901 Western Ave, Seattle, WA

Join Ragamala Seattle for a family-friendly day of Indian music and art activities at the Seattle Asian Art Museum. 11 am-noon Enjoy the beats and rhythms of Indian classical music by participating in a drum circle led by Ravi Albright of the Seattle Tabla Institute. noon-1 pm Learn to sing Hindustani vocal music in this beginner-friendly workshop led by teaching artists Suchitra Iyer and Anindita Das 2-4 pm Enjoy a concert and performance from celebrated Sitar player Pt. Kushal Das accompanied by Shri. Sandip Ghosh on Tabla. Pt. Kushal Das, renowned Sitar and Surbahar artist, was born in a musical family in Kolkata. His father Sailen Das was a disciple of Lakshman Bhattacharya and Pt. Ravi Shankar. His uncle Santanu Das was a disciple of Pt. Ali Akbar Khan. He started his training under the guidance of his father and uncle. Later he received training from Sanjoy Bandopadhyay and from Ajoy Sinha Roy \(a disciple of Pt. Allauddin Khan\) and took vocal lessons from Pt. Manas Chakraborty and Pt. Ramkrishna Basu. Pt. Das will be accompanied on the tabla by Sandip Ghosh, who has been a disciple of Pt. Anindo Chatterjee for the last 18 years. Sandip has accompanied distinguished artists like Pt. Ajoy Chakrabarty, Ud. Shahid Parvez, Ud. Shujaat Khan, Pt. Hari Prasad Chaurasia and Vid. Kaushiki Chakrabarty. He is also a solo artist, collaborating with bands worldwide.

item.title: Bartender's Circle Summit 2026 - Seattle Cocktail Week
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Pacific Place Mall, 600 Pine St., Seattle, WA

The world’s bar community is converging on Seattle. For three days, we are transforming the iconic Pacific Place into a multi-floor global campus for the hospitality elite. This isn’t just a regional seminar—it’s where the industry meets to define the next era of cocktail culture.Whether you are traveling from across the ocean or across the city, the Summit offers a masterclass in professional evolution, led by the most influential voices in the game.️ Learn From the LegendsWe’ve curated an unparalleled roster of international talent. Join these icons for deep-dives into the craft:Ivy Mix: Global bar owner and co-founder of Speed Rack.Jeffrey Morgenthaler: Renowned author and master of modern bar management.Camper English: The world’s leading "cocktail scientist" and expert on spirits physics.Jim Romdall: Seattle’s industry veteran on sustainability and long-term bar business....plus dozens more global ambassadors and master distillers. Three Tracks to MasteryOur curriculum is strategically divided into three pillars of excellence, allowing you to customize your education based on your career goals:The Skills Track: Precision meets innovation. These hands-on labs and technical sessions focus on the "how" behind world-class drinks—covering everything from advanced ice programs and molecular science to creative menu development and speed of service.The Spirits Track: This is for the true student of the glass. Go deep into the technical heritage of global categories like Cognac and Pisco, explore distillation secrets with master producers, and refine your palate with curated tastings of rare expressions and new releases.The Business Track: Build a career that lasts. These sessions tackle the "why" and "how much" of the industry—focusing on bar operations, sustainable business models, hospitality leadership, and maintaining physical and mental wellness for a long-term career.️ Secure Your Global AccessThe Summit Class Pass - $45The International Professional’s Choice Full 3-day access to all educational tracks \(Skills, Spirits, Business\) at Pacific Place. Includes entry to exclusive industry lounges and global networking sessions.The Pro-Bundle \(Summit \+ Carnival of Cocktails\) - $75The Ultimate Experience | Best Value Maximize your trip to Seattle. This combo deal is for those who want the full educational foundation plus the high-energy celebration of the Saturday Carnival.Full 3-Day Summit Access: Every class, every speaker, every track.Saturday Carnival Entry: The PNW’s largest spirits tasting event at Pacific Place.12 Tasting Tokens: Sample 100\+ brands and immersive pop-ups.The Bundle Discount: Get the best industry rate by pairing your education and your celebration.

item.title: Bosanko Public Lecture on Sustainability and Growth in Developing Economies
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Northwest Worklofts (Commons), 107 West Denny Way, Seattle, WA

Seattle University’s Albers School of Business and Economics invites the public to a Bosanko Lecture featuring Aparna Sawhney, Professor at the Centre for the Study of the World Economy in the School of International Studies at Jawaharlal Nehru University \(India\). In this academic public lecture, Professor Sawhney will examine India’s clean energy transition through the lens of sustainability, economic growth, and energy security in a developing-economy context. As one of the world’s fastest-growing economies and home to nearly 1.5 billion people, India faces the complex challenge of expanding energy access while reducing carbon emissions and promoting sustainable development. The lecture will explore India’s renewable energy policies, its experience with tradeable clean energy credits, and emerging approaches to carbon pricing within its broader development strategy. Attendees will gain insight into how policy design, market mechanisms, and international trade intersect with climate goals in developing countries. The lecture will be followed by a moderated Q&A session. This event is open to students, faculty, researchers, policy professionals, and members of the public interested in climate policy, sustainable development, and global energy transitions.

item.title: Smarty Pints! Plants, Pollinators, and Cannabis Chemistry
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Burke-Gilman Brewing Company, 3626 NE 45th St, Suite 102, Seattle, WA

Spring has sprung, so we’re bringing you pollinators and weed\(s\) for Smarty Pints this month! On April 20 we’re back with more timely talks, on how changing snow conditions affect plants and their pollinators and on the chemistry of cannabis, beer, and essential oils. See you at 6:30 in the taproom! 🐝Prof. Berry Brosi Professor, UW Biology Executive Director, MeadoWatch Climate-driven plant-pollinator timing mismatches: does biodiversity provide backup? Pollination is essential for plant reproduction, biodiversity, and agriculture. Scientists are increasingly worried about timing mismatches between plants and their pollinators, driven by climate change. Plants and insects use different environmental cues \(temperature, day length, snowmelt\) to time when they bloom and when they become active, and climate change is increasingly causing these cues to fall out of sync. Most research on this problem has focused on individual plants in isolation, which tend to show negative effects from mistimed pollination. But in nature, most plants are visited by multiple pollinator species and vice versa—so could that biodiversity act as a buffer against these mismatches? To find out, we conducted a study in the Colorado Rockies, where snowmelt largely controls when plants bloom. We experimentally manipulated snowmelt in large plots containing multiple plant species and many individuals of each. Come to the talk to find out what we discovered about how pollinators responded—and whether the plant pollination suffered! 🧪 Jim Roe Analytical Chemist, SoRSE Technology Volatile compounds in cannabis, essential oils, and beer What’s the chemistry behind your favorite smokes, scents, and suds? We’ll discuss terpenes and other -enes, -ones, and -ols that you definitely did not forget after that o-chem final back in college. Jim Roe has made a career out of chemistry instrumentation and analysis, from a research scientist managing the UW Chemistry department’s instrument lab to a field service engineer for equipment spanning the alphabet \(GCMS, HPLC, FTIR… holler for your favorite\). He has consulted on setting up cannabis laboratories around the Pacific Northwest, and currently works in research and development for water-soluble emulsions of cannabinoids, terpenes, hop oils, and other compounds used in food, beverages, and topicals.

item.title: EMBODIED ALTARS DANCE CLASS
item.description:
Monday, April 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA

EMBODIED ALTARS is an all-levels dance class hosted by Daybreak Star Radio. Designed by Indigenous Chicana artist and El Sueño director, Alicia Mullikin. Dancers will utilize Indigenous principles within movement, meditation, and song as a way to practice gratitude, honor the land, and recognize their ancestors. The class is for those who are interested in reflection, intention setting, and a discovery of their own stories. No dance experience necessary. Class Time: Mondays, 6:30-8PM No class 9/15/25 No class 10/13/25 Venue: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way, Seattle, WA 98199 Sliding Scale: $10-$20\* per class. Sign Up Now!   This venue is ADA accessible w/ ADA bathrooms and a wheelchair lift to the second floor. Class will be held in the ballroom space, this area has 5 ADA parking spaces. There are an additional 25 parking spots in our lot, there is ample parking space along the road leading up to Daybreak star as well as the Discovery Park North Parking lot. What to bring: water, comfortable clothes to dance in, journal, your personal identity, a desire to explore new pathways and an open mind/heart. Hosted at Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center Produced by United Indians of All Tribes Foundation In Partnership with El Sueno Dance & Daybreak Star Radio “Indigenize the Airwaves” “Art. Culture. Reclamation.” \*Talk to us about BIPOC and Queer/LGBTQIA2S\+ Scholorships\* Please contact @el.sueno.dance on IG for private conversations about this topic.

item.title: Legislative and Transportation Committee Meeting
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: SCERA, 625 S. 4th Street, Renton, WA

The Legislative and Transportation committee supports chamber membership by advocating for sound business practices at state and local government levels. This includes, advocacy in Olympia, developing policy positions, legislation receptions, review referendums and initiatives, and help organize all legislation events of the Chamber. The committee fulfills this by the following methods: Chamber Day planning, attending and messaging to our elected officials Another day in Olympia to advocate for small business issues given bills proposed, dropped and in process. Ongoing bulletin messages on proposed legislation and tax impacts to business/our members. Transportation presentations throughout the year on changes, tolling, etc., and communicate to members. Work and develop policy positions with the South KC Coalition that speak to our member’s business needs. This requires ongoing communication with the coalition and business members. Prioritized Renton Chamber legislative priorities and impact to business. Legislative Reception to enable Chamber members to hear from incumbents and candidates. Invite all individuals running for election to ask for Chamber endorsement. Given responses to that and interviews completed by the committee, recommend the committee’s decision to the board for their input/approval and then communication. Review referendums, initiatives and local issues as appropriate for recommendation to the Board. Sponsor the Legislative Reception in the fall to introduce candidates to local business. FREE TO ATTEND \(Sponsorships are availabe to support this committee\)

Here is where you learn to use the SUPER 6 Tools, when you log in to your MIC \(Member Information Center\). Maximize Your Membership 101 workshop is designed to educate our valued members on the benefits of using your Chamber Master access. We will also cover networking opportunities and advocacy that are available to you as a member of the Renton Chamber of Commerce. This training session will equip you with the ability to navigate and utilize your MIC Tools to effectively promote your business reaching over 10k potential customers. Posting Jobs, Events, Member 2 Member and Hot Deals, and more, will be shown. Our membership is similar to a gym... the more you use it, the better the results! We also welcome businesses who are interested in chamber membership to join us! FREE

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Book-It Repertory Theatre, 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, WA

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session\\\*\*\* Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate \(paper, t-shirts, etc\) - Water based ink Vera \*does not\* allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. \*\*FAQ\*\* 1. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

item.title: China and India: Conflict and Cooperation in the (De)Globalization Era
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Northwest Worklofts (Commons), 107 West Denny Way, Seattle, WA

The 2026 Peter L. Lee Endowed Lecture in East Asian Culture and Civilization will be given by T.V. Paul, Distinguished James McGill Professor of the Political Science Department at McGill University. 2026 Peter L. Lee Endowed Lecture in East Asian Culture and Civilization Speaker: T.V. Paul Location: Casey Commons 4:00 pm Reception 4:30-6:00 pm Lecture Free and open to Students, Faculty and Community Members Please register to RSVP if interested China and India: Conflict and Cooperation in the \(De\)Globalization Era The relations between Asian giants China and India have been characterized by a mixture of conflict and cooperation. Since the 1962 territorial dispute, there have been intermittent flare-ups with no clear end in sight. However, India and China seem to agree on many issues such as trade, global financial reforms and climate change. This talk explores the multifaceted causes of the peculiar contours of this “managed rivalry.” A broad range of topics such as territory dispute, status, resources, conceptions of regional and world order, strategic culture, balance of power and deterrence strategies will be analyzed. The mitigating role of asymmetrical economic interdependence adopted by the two states as well as their common membership in institutions such as BRICS, SCO and G20 have helped periodic crises from escalating into wars. The China-India relationship is likely to shape 21st century international politics and the Indo-Pacific regional order profoundly.

Event details

item.title: An Evening with Amy Cordalis
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Sinegal Center for Science and Innovation, 200, Seattle, WA

Join us for an evening with Amy Bowers Cordalis, a Yurok mother, fisherwoman, attorney, and nationally recognized leader in Indigenous environmental advocacy. Amy has served as General Counsel of the Yurok Nation and now leads the Ridges to Riffles Indigenous Conservation Group, where she works to advance sovereignty and protect cultural and natural resources, including the undamming of the Klamath River. Her leadership has earned global recognition, including the United Nations Champions of the World Laureate and a place on the 2024 TIME100 Climate List honoring the most influential voices in climate action. She is also the author of The Water Remembers \(Hachette, 2025\) and will have copies of the book available for purchase at the event. Open to the public at no cost, this event offers an opportunity to engage with a powerful voice for land, water, and Indigenous futures. Early registration is encouraged, as space and tickets are limited. Co-sponsored with the Institute for Catholic Thought and Culture and the Center for Environmental Justice and Sustainability.

item.title: Type Design Basics
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview So you want to design a typeface? Learn how to hand-draw letters and digitize them to create your own unique functional font! In this practical workshop, students will create an original text typeface based on traditional European calligraphy. Whether you want to develop your typography skills, improve your lettering practice, or become a type designer, learning the fundamentals of type design can bring your skills to the next level. Explore both analog media \(pen on paper\) and digital media \(computer\). Learn how to use a calligraphy pen to develop an understanding of the underlying structure of the Latin alphabet. Then you can work on improving your drawings digitally, and create a typeface using Glyphs \(or the font editor of your choice\).   Class time is dedicated to working on your project with individualized feedback, supplemented by lectures, technical demonstrations, and group critique. By the end of the class, you will have a working text typeface of your own creation, as well as a sketch-to-screen workflow that you can use for future font creation. Students will be encouraged to spend up to 10 hours on homework outside of class time. Designing a typeface is a laborious process, but time flies when experimenting, drawing, and editing! This class is offered in partnership with the amazing Seattle Creative Academy \(but this class is not part of their program and is not for credit/degree\)! See also: Experimental Lettering and other Graphic Design or art classes on our website. Learning Objectives Learn the basics of calligraphy Understand the structure and patterning of the Latin alphabet Create your own original letterforms Digitize and refine your original design Learn how to optimize a typeface for text Become familiar with a sketch-to-screen digital type design workflow Who Might Enjoy This Class? Anyone who wants to develop their own custom font Graphic designers, illustrators, lettering artists, type designers -- anyone with related experience who would like to hone their type design skills Architects, engineers, artists, crafters, writers, book lovers -- anyone interested in the form of letters who would enjoy creating and using their own handmade font No prerequisites, but basic graphic design/typography knowledge is recommended

item.title: Bartender's Circle Summit 2026 - Seattle Cocktail Week
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: Pacific Place Mall, 600 Pine St., Seattle, WA

The world’s bar community is converging on Seattle. For three days, we are transforming the iconic Pacific Place into a multi-floor global campus for the hospitality elite. This isn’t just a regional seminar—it’s where the industry meets to define the next era of cocktail culture.Whether you are traveling from across the ocean or across the city, the Summit offers a masterclass in professional evolution, led by the most influential voices in the game.️ Learn From the LegendsWe’ve curated an unparalleled roster of international talent. Join these icons for deep-dives into the craft:Ivy Mix: Global bar owner and co-founder of Speed Rack.Jeffrey Morgenthaler: Renowned author and master of modern bar management.Camper English: The world’s leading "cocktail scientist" and expert on spirits physics.Jim Romdall: Seattle’s industry veteran on sustainability and long-term bar business....plus dozens more global ambassadors and master distillers. Three Tracks to MasteryOur curriculum is strategically divided into three pillars of excellence, allowing you to customize your education based on your career goals:The Skills Track: Precision meets innovation. These hands-on labs and technical sessions focus on the "how" behind world-class drinks—covering everything from advanced ice programs and molecular science to creative menu development and speed of service.The Spirits Track: This is for the true student of the glass. Go deep into the technical heritage of global categories like Cognac and Pisco, explore distillation secrets with master producers, and refine your palate with curated tastings of rare expressions and new releases.The Business Track: Build a career that lasts. These sessions tackle the "why" and "how much" of the industry—focusing on bar operations, sustainable business models, hospitality leadership, and maintaining physical and mental wellness for a long-term career.️ Secure Your Global AccessThe Summit Class Pass - $45The International Professional’s Choice Full 3-day access to all educational tracks \(Skills, Spirits, Business\) at Pacific Place. Includes entry to exclusive industry lounges and global networking sessions.The Pro-Bundle \(Summit \+ Carnival of Cocktails\) - $75The Ultimate Experience | Best Value Maximize your trip to Seattle. This combo deal is for those who want the full educational foundation plus the high-energy celebration of the Saturday Carnival.Full 3-Day Summit Access: Every class, every speaker, every track.Saturday Carnival Entry: The PNW’s largest spirits tasting event at Pacific Place.12 Tasting Tokens: Sample 100\+ brands and immersive pop-ups.The Bundle Discount: Get the best industry rate by pairing your education and your celebration.

item.title: Family Business Exchange | Seminar: Seattle Chocolate (now Maeve)
item.description:
Wednesday, April 22, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Casey Building, 530, Seattle, WA

The Family Business Exchange welcomes Jean Thompson, CEO and Owner of Seattle Chocolate Company, and her daughter, Ellie Thompson, now the second generation in the business, for a fireside chat to share their unique journey together. We'll hear Jean's reflections on her unexpected path into entrepreneurship and how her leadership sparked Ellie’s interest in joining the company. Together, they have navigated family-business dynamics and embraced bold decisions—most notably Ellie’s leadership as Director of Marketing in the recent rebranding to “Maeve.” Hear candid insights from both perspectives on lessons learned, strategies for fostering leadership growth, and a vision for the future that blends legacy with innovation—takeaways every family business leader can apply. About the Family Business Exchange Seattle University’s Family Business Exchange \(FBX\) is a community of Pacific Northwest family business owners who gather to engage in education, networking, and professional development tailored specifically to their needs. FBX provides opportunities for families to address their unique challenges through relevant programming. It serves as a forum for information exchange with the goal of helping each member thrive through future generations. Areas of best practice that we cover include family business succession planning, governance, leadership development and growth, among others. About Equinox Business Law Group Equinox Business Law Group provides strategic legal counsel for entrepreneurs at a fixed monthly cost. Founded by Michelle Bomberger, Equinox brings two decades of legal expertise to entrepreneurs. As business owners themselves, they combine legal knowledge with a powerful network of industry experts to support your business growth. Equinox is a proud Corporate Member of Seattle University’s Family Business Exchange.

College Possible Washington provides intensive college access and success programming for students from low-income backgrounds and those who are the first in their families to attend college. item.title: Contemporary Dance for Every Body
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Would you like to cultivate body awareness through movement? Explore a grounded and natural approach to moving your body, led by a contemporary dance instructor. Through floorwork, movement sequences, and phrasing, this class cultivates body awareness. Explore efficiency in movement transitions. Develop a deeper understanding of how your body moves naturally in relation to the floor, emphasizing organic flow, continuity, spirality, softness, core engagement, and the intelligent use of gravity. Open your mind and body through guided movement! Explore points of support, releasing tension, and engaging the core to facilitate flow and prevent injury. Movement principles will draw from contemporary dance and somatic practices, encouraging dancers to move with clarity, sensitivity, and presence. This class is for dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners, and anyone interested in exploring movement in their bodies -- no experience necessary. Designed to be inclusive, this class offers both accessibility and challenge to movers at various levels and all backgrounds.  Learning Objectives Understand and embody key principles of movement floorwork, including gravity, spirality, and core initiation. Improve movement continuity and flow between levels \(floor to standing and vice versa\). Explore softness and efficiency through core engagement. Identify and use anatomical points of support for safe and fluid movement. Develop new dance tools Feel more connected with the possibilities of your body in a conscious way to live a more present, healthy, and aware life. Who Might Enjoy This Class? Adult learners of all backgrounds \(18\+\), including dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners Anyone interested in embodied movement practices. Some prior movement or dance experience is helpful but not required. The class will be structured to accommodate varying levels of experience and physical ability.

item.title: Housing Justice: From Policy to Practice
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 12 PM

Join YWCA Seattle | King | Snohomish for Housing Justice: From Policy to Practice, a virtual panel focusing on the ways housing policies show up in real life. We will explore what's working and what's not, and the changes needed to ensure safe, stable, and affordable housing for all people across King and Snohomish Counties. Hear from a group of community leaders, advocates, and service providers, as they ground the conversation in local context, share the realities of implementation, and elevate solutions that center equity, accountability, lived experience, and community voice. Featured speakers for this event are: - Gregory Davis, Rainier Beach Action Coalition - Colleen Echohawk, Community Roots Housing - Alison Eisinger, Seattle/King County Coalition on Homelessness - Estela Ortega, El Centro de la Raza - Mindy Woods, Snohomish County Human Services \(facilitator\) About Until Justice Just Is This year marks the 15th anniversary of this annual event, formerly known as Stand Against Racism and hosted by the People of Color Executive Council of YWCA Seattle | King | Snohomish. A signature campaign of YWCA USA Until Justice Just Is invites communities across the country to come together with YWCA to raise awareness about the impacts of racism and to build community among those working for racial justice.
